meta.title: Taxonomy of the Plant Kingdom
meta.description: Hierarchical classification of land plants (Embryophyta) from kingdom down to family. Vascular plants are resolved to family; bryophytes to class.
meta.note: Flat source of truth: one record per taxon, keyed by `id`, parent pointer in `parent`. build.py derives the nested tree. Enrich with build/fetch.py.
meta.fields: id, parent, rank, name, common?, speciesCount?, examples?[], blurb?, ids{}, provenance{}

wcvp.name: World Checklist of Vascular Plants (WCVP), Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew

megatree.name: Dated plant megatree (GBOTB.extended): Jin & Qian 2022; Smith & Brown 2018; Zanne et al. 2014

taxa.name: Haplomitriopsida
taxa.blurb: Earliest-diverging liverworts with fleshy leafy or thalloid shoots and simple oil-body cells; ~30 species.

taxa.name: Marchantiopsida
taxa.blurb: Flat ribbon-like thalli with air chambers and pores, often bearing umbrella-shaped gametophores.

taxa.name: Jungermanniopsida
taxa.blurb: Largest liverwort class, mostly leafy shoots with lobed leaves in ranks; ~85% of all liverwort species.

taxa.name: Takakiopsida
taxa.blurb: Tiny moss with cylindrical finger-like leaf segments, once mistaken for a liverwort; only two species.

taxa.name: Sphagnopsida
taxa.blurb: Bog-forming mosses with water-storing hyaline cells that build vast peatlands and store carbon.

taxa.name: Andreaeopsida
taxa.blurb: Small blackish rock mosses whose capsules split into four lantern-like valves; grow on acidic granite.

taxa.name: Andreaeobryopsida
taxa.blurb: Single-species class of calcareous-rock mosses of northwestern North America with capsules on a stalk.

taxa.name: Oedipodiopsida
taxa.blurb: Monotypic class of a delicate moss with long-necked capsules lacking a peristome; found in cool regions.

taxa.name: Tetraphidopsida
taxa.blurb: Small mosses whose capsules bear a distinctive peristome of just four large teeth.

taxa.name: Polytrichopsida
taxa.blurb: Robust mosses with leaves bearing photosynthetic lamellae and conducting tissue; includes tallest mosses.

taxa.name: Bryopsida
taxa.blurb: Largest moss class with jointed, twisting peristome teeth; ~95% of all moss species worldwide.

taxa.name: Leiosporocerotopsida
taxa.blurb: Monotypic hornwort class notable for smooth spores and internal channels housing symbiotic cyanobacteria.

taxa.name: Anthocerotopsida
taxa.blurb: Bryophytes with flat thalli and needle-like sporophyte horns that grow from a persistent basal meristem.

taxa.name: Lycopodiaceae
taxa.blurb: Ancient vascular plants with small needle-like leaves and spores borne in cone-like strobili.

taxa.name: Isoetaceae
taxa.blurb: Aquatic or amphibious lycophytes with a corm and quill-like leaves whose bases hold spores.

taxa.name: Selaginellaceae
taxa.blurb: Creeping lycophytes with scale-like leaves and two spore sizes; some are desert resurrection plants.

taxa.name: Equisetaceae
taxa.blurb: Jointed, hollow, silica-rich stems with whorled branches and terminal cone; the sole living horsetail family.

taxa.name: Psilotaceae
taxa.blurb: Rootless ferns with forking green stems and reduced leaves, bearing fused three-lobed sporangia.

taxa.name: Ophioglossaceae
taxa.blurb: Fleshy ferns with a single divided frond and a separate spore-bearing spike; have the highest known chromosome counts.

taxa.name: Marattiaceae
taxa.blurb: Large tropical eusporangiate ferns with massive fleshy fronds and paired sporangial synangia.

taxa.name: Osmundaceae
taxa.blurb: Ancient ferns with large fronds and distinct fertile pinnae; leave a rich fossil record.

taxa.name: Hymenophyllaceae
taxa.blurb: Delicate ferns with translucent blades one cell thick, growing in constantly humid, shaded habitats.

taxa.name: Gleicheniaceae
taxa.blurb: Scrambling ferns with repeatedly forking fronds that form dense thickets in open tropical sites.

taxa.name: Dipteridaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical ferns with fan-shaped, dichotomously veined fronds; a small relict family.

taxa.name: Matoniaceae
taxa.blurb: Rare Malesian ferns with pedately branched fronds and umbrella-covered sori; relicts of a once-widespread group.

taxa.name: Lygodiaceae
taxa.blurb: Twining ferns whose indeterminate frond rachis climbs like a vine, sometimes to several meters.

taxa.name: Schizaeaceae
taxa.blurb: Ferns with narrow grass-like or fan-shaped fronds and comb-like fertile spikes.

taxa.name: Anemiaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly American ferns bearing conspicuous erect fertile pinnae that resemble flower spikes.

taxa.name: Salviniaceae
taxa.blurb: Free-floating aquatic ferns; Azolla hosts nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria and is used as a rice-paddy fertilizer.

taxa.name: Marsileaceae
taxa.blurb: Rooted aquatic ferns with clover-like or grass-like leaves and spores enclosed in hard sporocarps.

taxa.name: Thyrsopteridaceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic tree-fern family endemic to the Juan Fernandez Islands, with cup-shaped fertile segments.

taxa.name: Loxsomataceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of terrestrial ferns from New Zealand and the Andes with flask-shaped marginal sori.

taxa.name: Culcitaceae
taxa.blurb: Ferns with hairy creeping rhizomes and cup-like marginal sori, allied to the tree ferns.

taxa.name: Plagiogyriaceae
taxa.blurb: Terrestrial ferns lacking scales, with dimorphic fronds and swollen aerating pneumathodes on the stipe.

taxa.name: Cibotiaceae
taxa.blurb: Tree ferns with trunks clothed in soft golden hairs; Hawaiian species are iconic rainforest plants.

taxa.name: Metaxyaceae
taxa.blurb: Neotropical ferns with creeping rhizomes and once-pinnate fronds bearing rounded sori without indusia.

taxa.name: Dicksoniaceae
taxa.blurb: Tree ferns with stout hairy trunks and marginal cup-shaped sori; widely grown as garden tree ferns.

taxa.name: Cyatheaceae
taxa.blurb: The dominant tree-fern family, with tall scaly trunks and huge fronds forming forest canopies.

taxa.name: Saccolomataceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical terrestrial ferns with large divided fronds and pouch-like marginal sori.

taxa.name: Cystodiaceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic family of a large Malesian forest fern with hairy rhizomes and pouch-shaped sori.

taxa.name: Lonchitidaceae
taxa.blurb: Small pantropical family of terrestrial ferns with large fronds and elongate marginal sori.

taxa.name: Lindsaeaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly tropical ferns with wedge-shaped pinnae and pouch-like sori opening toward the leaf margin.

taxa.name: Pteridaceae
taxa.blurb: Large, diverse family including maidenhair, brake and cliff ferns; sori usually run along leaf margins.

taxa.name: Dennstaedtiaceae
taxa.blurb: Terrestrial ferns with creeping rhizomes; includes bracken, one of the world's most widespread plants.

taxa.name: Cystopteridaceae
taxa.blurb: Delicate temperate ferns of rocks and woodlands with hood-like indusia over the sori.

taxa.name: Rhachidosoraceae
taxa.blurb: Small East Asian family of large terrestrial ferns of limestone habitats with linear sori.

taxa.name: Diplaziopsidaceae
taxa.blurb: Terrestrial ferns with simply pinnate fleshy fronds and elongate sori along the veins.

taxa.name: Desmophlebiaceae
taxa.blurb: Small Neotropical family segregated from allied ferns, with distinctive connecting veins along the sori.

taxa.name: Hemidictyaceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic Neotropical family of large ferns with once-pinnate fronds and netted veins near the midrib.

taxa.name: Aspleniaceae
taxa.blurb: Widespread ferns, often on rocks or as epiphytes, with linear sori set along the veins.

taxa.name: Woodsiaceae
taxa.blurb: Small tufted ferns of rocky, often alpine habitats, with sori wrapped in a distinctive fringed indusium.

taxa.name: Onocleaceae
taxa.blurb: Ferns with strongly dimorphic fronds; separate bead-like fertile fronds enclose the sori.

taxa.name: Blechnaceae
taxa.blurb: Ferns often with reddish young fronds and linear sori in chain-like rows parallel to the midrib.

taxa.name: Athyriaceae
taxa.blurb: Soft-textured woodland ferns with elongate, often J-shaped or hooked sori along the veins.

taxa.name: Thelypteridaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly terrestrial ferns with needle-like hairs and rounded sori; common in wetlands and forests.

taxa.name: Didymochlaenaceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic pantropical family of a large fern with bipinnate fronds and paired oblong sori.

taxa.name: Hypodematiaceae
taxa.blurb: Ferns of rocky, often limestone habitats with scaly swollen rhizome bases and kidney-shaped indusia.

taxa.name: Dryopteridaceae
taxa.blurb: Large family of terrestrial and epiphytic ferns, typically with round sori under kidney-shaped indusia.

taxa.name: Nephrolepidaceae
taxa.blurb: Ferns with once-pinnate fronds spreading by runners; includes the popular Boston fern houseplant.

taxa.name: Lomariopsidaceae
taxa.blurb: Often climbing or root-clinging tropical ferns with dimorphic fronds and sori covering the fertile blade.

taxa.name: Tectariaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly tropical terrestrial ferns with netted veins and round sori, often with variably shaped fronds.

taxa.name: Oleandraceae
taxa.blurb: Epiphytic or climbing ferns with wiry scaly stems and simple, undivided lance-shaped fronds.

taxa.name: Davalliaceae
taxa.blurb: Epiphytic ferns with furry creeping rhizomes and finely divided fronds; grown as ornamental basket ferns.

taxa.name: Polypodiaceae
taxa.blurb: Largely epiphytic ferns with creeping rhizomes and round sori lacking indusia; includes staghorn ferns.

taxa.name: Cycadaceae
taxa.blurb: Palm-like evergreen gymnosperms with stout trunks and stiff pinnate leaves; the sole genus Cycas bears seeds on leaf-like megasporophylls.

taxa.name: Zamiaceae
taxa.blurb: Cone-bearing cycads with palm- or fern-like foliage and coralloid nitrogen-fixing roots; many are long-lived and highly threatened.

taxa.name: Ginkgoaceae
taxa.blurb: A living-fossil lineage with one surviving species, the deciduous fan-leaved ginkgo, sole remnant of an ancient gymnosperm order.

taxa.name: Ephedraceae
taxa.blurb: Jointed, broom-like desert shrubs with scale leaves; source of the alkaloid ephedrine and long used medicinally as ma huang.

taxa.name: Gnetaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical woody vines, shrubs and trees with broad angiosperm-like leaves; some yield edible seeds and leaves (melinjo).

taxa.name: Welwitschiaceae
taxa.blurb: A single bizarre Namib Desert species growing just two ever-lengthening strap leaves for a lifespan of over a thousand years.

taxa.name: Pinaceae
taxa.blurb: Resinous cone-bearing evergreens including pines, firs, spruces and larches; dominant timber trees of northern-hemisphere forests.

taxa.name: Araucariaceae
taxa.blurb: Ancient southern-hemisphere conifers, often massive, including the monkey puzzle, kauri and living-fossil Wollemi pine.

taxa.name: Podocarpaceae
taxa.blurb: Largely southern-hemisphere conifers bearing seeds on fleshy, berry-like receptacles; a major evergreen family of austral forests.

taxa.name: Sciadopityaceae
taxa.blurb: One relict Japanese conifer species whose flattened photosynthetic shoots radiate like the ribs of an umbrella.

taxa.name: Cupressaceae
taxa.blurb: Scale- or awl-leaved conifers spanning junipers, cypresses and the giant redwoods, among the tallest and oldest living trees.

taxa.name: Taxaceae
taxa.blurb: Evergreen conifers with soft flat needles whose seeds sit in a fleshy red aril; source of the anticancer drug taxol.

taxa.name: Amborellaceae
taxa.blurb: A single shrub endemic to New Caledonia, sister lineage to all other flowering plants and lacking water-conducting vessels.

taxa.name: Hydatellaceae
taxa.blurb: Tiny annual aquatic herbs of ephemeral pools, once mistaken for sedges but now placed among the earliest-diverging angiosperms.

taxa.name: Cabombaceae
taxa.blurb: Rooted freshwater plants with submerged fan-shaped or floating shield leaves; Cabomba is a popular aquarium oxygenator.

taxa.name: Nymphaeaceae
taxa.blurb: Rooted aquatic herbs with floating leaves and showy flowers, including the giant Amazon water lily Victoria.

taxa.name: Austrobaileyaceae
taxa.blurb: A single woody climbing vine of Queensland rainforest, an early-branching angiosperm with foul-smelling, spotted flowers.

taxa.name: Trimeniaceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic climbers and shrubs of Australasian and Pacific rainforests, representing a basal angiosperm lineage of the Austrobaileyales.

taxa.name: Schisandraceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic woody vines and shrubs; includes the spice star anise and Schisandra berries used in East Asian medicine.

taxa.name: Canellaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical trees with aromatic bark used as a cinnamon-like spice and in traditional medicine.

taxa.name: Winteraceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic southern trees and shrubs with primitive vessel-less wood; Drimys bark was a historic scurvy remedy.

taxa.name: Aristolochiaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and vines with pipe-shaped, often fly-pollinated flowers; many contain toxic aristolochic acids.

taxa.name: Saururaceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic wetland herbs with slender spikes of petal-less flowers; Houttuynia is used as a herb and groundcover.

taxa.name: Piperaceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic herbs, shrubs and vines with tiny flowers on dense spikes; source of black pepper, kava and popular Peperomia houseplants.

taxa.name: Myristicaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical aromatic trees with red-arillate seeds; Myristica yields both the spices nutmeg and mace.

taxa.name: Magnoliaceae
taxa.blurb: Trees and shrubs with large solitary flowers of many spirally arranged parts, considered an ancient angiosperm floral form.

taxa.name: Degeneriaceae
taxa.blurb: Two rainforest tree species endemic to Fiji, noted for primitive unsealed carpels and undifferentiated stamens.

taxa.name: Himantandraceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic rainforest trees of Australasia with scaly bud coverings, containing psychoactive and medicinal alkaloids.

taxa.name: Eupomatiaceae
taxa.blurb: A few Australasian shrubs and small trees whose petal-less flowers are pollinated by weevils drawn to sterile inner staminodes.

taxa.name: Annonaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical trees, shrubs and lianas bearing aromatic edible fruits such as soursop and pawpaw, plus the perfume tree ylang-ylang.

taxa.name: Calycanthaceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic shrubs with fragrant, spirally arranged flower parts; includes sweetshrub and winter-blooming wintersweet.

taxa.name: Siparunaceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic Neotropical and West African shrubs and trees with flowers enclosed in a cup, used in traditional remedies.

taxa.name: Gomortegaceae
taxa.blurb: A single endangered aromatic tree endemic to central Chile, bearing edible yellow drupes.

taxa.name: Atherospermataceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic evergreen trees of southern-hemisphere temperate forests, some yielding fragrant, spicy bark and timber.

taxa.name: Hernandiaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical trees, shrubs and vines with winged or lantern-like fruits adapted for wind or water dispersal.

taxa.name: Monimiaceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic southern-hemisphere trees and shrubs; the Chilean boldo yields leaves used as a herbal tea and digestive remedy.

taxa.name: Lauraceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly aromatic evergreen trees and shrubs yielding bay leaves, cinnamon, camphor and the avocado fruit.

taxa.name: Chloranthaceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic herbs and shrubs with swollen leaf nodes and tiny reduced flowers; an ancient lineage rich in the fossil record.

taxa.name: Acoraceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Acorus of aromatic rhizomatous wetland herbs, sister to all other monocots.

taxa.name: Araceae
taxa.blurb: ~3,700 species with a fleshy spadix wrapped by a spathe, including duckweeds, the world's smallest flowering plants.

taxa.name: Tofieldiaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of bog-dwelling rhizomatous herbs; Triantha includes a recently confirmed carnivorous species.

taxa.name: Alismataceae
taxa.blurb: Aquatic and marsh herbs with whorled flowers and often arrow-shaped emergent leaves.

taxa.name: Butomaceae
taxa.blurb: Single species Butomus umbellatus, a rush-like emergent aquatic bearing umbels of pink flowers.

taxa.name: Hydrocharitaceae
taxa.blurb: Fully aquatic freshwater and marine herbs including submerged waterweeds and true seagrasses.

taxa.name: Scheuchzeriaceae
taxa.blurb: Single species Scheuchzeria palustris, a rhizomatous herb of northern-hemisphere peat bogs.

taxa.name: Aponogetonaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Aponogeton of freshwater aquatic herbs, several grown as aquarium plants.

taxa.name: Juncaginaceae
taxa.blurb: Rush-like herbs of marshes and salt-flats with slender leaves and spike-like inflorescences.

taxa.name: Maundiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single species Maundia triglochinoides, an emergent aquatic herb endemic to eastern Australia.

taxa.name: Zosteraceae
taxa.blurb: Marine seagrasses forming submerged meadows in temperate coastal waters, pollinated underwater.

taxa.name: Potamogetonaceae
taxa.blurb: Cosmopolitan submerged and floating-leaved freshwater herbs important as aquatic wildlife food.

taxa.name: Posidoniaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Posidonia of marine seagrasses forming vast, ancient Mediterranean and Australian meadows.

taxa.name: Ruppiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Ruppia of slender submerged herbs thriving in brackish and saline waters.

taxa.name: Cymodoceaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical and subtropical marine seagrasses forming meadows grazed by dugongs and manatees.

taxa.name: Petrosaviaceae
taxa.blurb: Tiny family including mycoheterotrophic herbs that lack chlorophyll and feed on soil fungi.

taxa.name: Nartheciaceae
taxa.blurb: Rhizomatous herbs of bogs and wet ground with grass-like leaves and yellow or white star flowers.

taxa.name: Burmanniaceae
taxa.blurb: Small often mycoheterotrophic herbs, many nearly leafless and lacking chlorophyll in shaded tropics.

taxa.name: Dioscoreaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly twining vines with starchy edible tubers; a staple food crop across the tropics.

taxa.name: Triuridaceae
taxa.blurb: Small mycoheterotrophic herbs lacking chlorophyll, with reduced scale leaves in tropical forest litter.

taxa.name: Velloziaceae
taxa.blurb: Woody desiccation-tolerant plants of rocky outcrops, dominant on Brazilian campo rupestre and African inselbergs.

taxa.name: Stemonaceae
taxa.blurb: Twining or erect herbs of Asia and the Pacific whose roots yield insecticidal alkaloids.

taxa.name: Cyclanthaceae
taxa.blurb: Neotropical palm-like herbs and vines; Carludovica leaves supply fiber for Panama hats.

taxa.name: Pandanaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical trees and shrubs with spiral leaf tufts and stilt roots, bearing pineapple-like fruits.

taxa.name: Campynemataceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of lily-like herbs restricted to Tasmania and New Caledonia.

taxa.name: Corsiaceae
taxa.blurb: Mycoheterotrophic herbs lacking chlorophyll, with one enlarged upper petal, from the Pacific and southern South America.

taxa.name: Melanthiaceae
taxa.blurb: Rhizomatous woodland and meadow herbs, many highly toxic, including trilliums and false hellebores.

taxa.name: Petermanniaceae
taxa.blurb: Single species Petermannia cirrosa, a prickly tendril-climbing vine of eastern Australian rainforest.

taxa.name: Alstroemeriaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and vines of the Americas with resupinate leaves; Alstroemeria is a major cut-flower crop.

taxa.name: Colchicaceae
taxa.blurb: Cormous and tuberous herbs producing colchicine; includes autumn crocus and climbing flame lilies.

taxa.name: Philesiaceae
taxa.blurb: Two Chilean genera of woody climbers and shrubs with showy red bell-shaped flowers.

taxa.name: Ripogonaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Ripogonum of woody climbers from Australasia, formerly placed in Smilacaceae.

taxa.name: Smilacaceae
taxa.blurb: Prickly tendril-bearing vines of woodlands; Smilax roots historically flavored sarsaparilla.

taxa.name: Liliaceae
taxa.blurb: Bulbous herbs with showy six-tepaled flowers, including true lilies, tulips and fritillaries.

taxa.name: Orchidaceae
taxa.blurb: The largest plant family, ~28,000 species of orchids, many epiphytic with intricate pollinator-specific flowers.

taxa.name: Boryaceae
taxa.blurb: Two Australian genera; Borya are desiccation-tolerant resurrection plants of seasonally dry rock.

taxa.name: Blandfordiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Blandfordia of eastern Australian herbs with pendulous red-and-yellow tubular flowers.

taxa.name: Asteliaceae
taxa.blurb: Tufted, often epiphytic herbs of the southern hemisphere with silvery grass-like leaves.

taxa.name: Lanariaceae
taxa.blurb: Single species Lanaria lanata, a woolly-flowered herb of the South African Cape fynbos.

taxa.name: Hypoxidaceae
taxa.blurb: Cormous or rhizomatous herbs with hairy leaves and yellow star-shaped flowers; some used medicinally.

taxa.name: Doryanthaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Doryanthes of giant Australian rosette herbs with tall spikes of red flowers.

taxa.name: Ixioliriaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Ixiolirion of bulbous steppe herbs from western and central Asia with blue funnel flowers.

taxa.name: Tecophilaeaceae
taxa.blurb: Cormous herbs of the Americas and Africa; includes the intensely blue, once-wild-extinct Chilean blue crocus.

taxa.name: Iridaceae
taxa.blurb: Rhizomatous, cormous or bulbous herbs with equitant leaves and three-parted flowers, including irises and crocuses.

taxa.name: Xeronemataceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Xeronema of rock-dwelling herbs from New Zealand and New Caledonia with one-sided red flower brushes.

taxa.name: Asphodelaceae
taxa.blurb: Succulent and herbaceous plants including aloes, daylilies and red-hot pokers, many with medicinal leaf gels.

taxa.name: Amaryllidaceae
taxa.blurb: Bulbous herbs including daffodils, snowdrops and the alliums; many contain alkaloids or pungent sulfur compounds.

taxa.name: Asparagaceae
taxa.blurb: Broad family of herbs, succulents and shrubs including edible asparagus, agaves, hyacinths and hostas.

taxa.name: Dasypogonaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of shrubby, tufted or tree-like perennials endemic to Australia; ~4 genera and 16 species.

taxa.name: Arecaceae
taxa.blurb: Palms: mostly tropical woody plants with unbranched trunks and large fan or feather leaves; ~2,600 species.

taxa.name: Hanguanaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus of dioecious rhizomatous herbs of tropical Asia and Australia, often growing in wet habitats.

taxa.name: Commelinaceae
taxa.blurb: Spiderworts: herbs with sheathing leaves and ephemeral three-petalled flowers lasting a day; ~730 species.

taxa.name: Philydraceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of marsh herbs from Asia and Australasia with yellow flowers and a single fertile stamen.

taxa.name: Pontederiaceae
taxa.blurb: Aquatic and marsh herbs with showy flowers; includes the invasive water hyacinth; ~35 species.

taxa.name: Haemodoraceae
taxa.blurb: Perennial herbs often with red root pigment; includes Australia's kangaroo paws; ~100 species.

taxa.name: Strelitziaceae
taxa.blurb: Large banana-like herbs and trees with striking beaked flowers; three genera including Strelitzia.

taxa.name: Lowiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus of rhizomatous herbs of tropical Asia with orchid-like, often carrion-scented flowers.

taxa.name: Heliconiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus of large tropical American herbs with vivid boat-shaped bracts pollinated by hummingbirds.

taxa.name: Musaceae
taxa.blurb: Bananas: giant herbs with pseudostems of rolled leaf sheaths bearing edible fruit; ~90 species.

taxa.name: Cannaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus of tropical American rhizomatous herbs with broad leaves and showy petal-like staminodes.

taxa.name: Marantaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical herbs with leaves that fold at night via a swollen pulvinus; includes arrowroot; ~550 species.

taxa.name: Costaceae
taxa.blurb: Spiral gingers: tropical herbs with spirally arranged leaves on cane-like stems; ~140 species.

taxa.name: Zingiberaceae
taxa.blurb: Gingers: aromatic rhizomatous herbs yielding ginger, turmeric and cardamom spices; ~1,600 species.

taxa.name: Typhaceae
taxa.blurb: Marsh and aquatic herbs with cylindrical spikes of tiny wind-pollinated flowers; includes cattails.

taxa.name: Bromeliaceae
taxa.blurb: Bromeliads: mostly tropical American herbs, many epiphytes forming water-holding rosettes; ~3,600 species.

taxa.name: Rapateaceae
taxa.blurb: Tufted herbs of tropical South American wetlands, with one outlier in West Africa; ~90 species.

taxa.name: Xyridaceae
taxa.blurb: Grass-like herbs of wet, sunny habitats with heads of short-lived yellow flowers; ~400 species.

taxa.name: Eriocaulaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs of wet habitats bearing button-like heads of tiny flowers on leafless stalks; ~1,200 species.

taxa.name: Mayacaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus of small aquatic or bog herbs with moss-like foliage, mostly from the Americas.

taxa.name: Thurniaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of tough rush-like herbs from South America and southern Africa; two genera.

taxa.name: Juncaceae
taxa.blurb: Rushes: grass-like herbs of wet, cool habitats with cylindrical stems and small dry flowers; ~460 species.

taxa.name: Cyperaceae
taxa.blurb: Sedges: grass-like herbs typically with solid, triangular stems and leaves in three ranks; ~5,500 species.

taxa.name: Restionaceae
taxa.blurb: Rush-like herbs of the Southern Hemisphere, dominant in South African fynbos and Australian heath.

taxa.name: Flagellariaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus of climbing tropical Old World reeds that scramble using coiling leaf-tip tendrils.

taxa.name: Joinvilleaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus of robust reed-like herbs of Pacific islands and Southeast Asia; close relatives of grasses.

taxa.name: Ecdeiocoleaceae
taxa.blurb: Tiny family of rush-like herbs endemic to Western Australia; among the closest relatives of true grasses.

taxa.name: Poaceae
taxa.blurb: Grasses: wind-pollinated herbs with jointed hollow stems; includes all cereal grains and bamboo; ~12,000 species.

taxa.name: Ceratophyllaceae
taxa.blurb: Rootless submerged aquatics with whorls of finely forked leaves; common pond and aquarium oxygenators.

taxa.name: Eupteleaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Euptelea: deciduous East Asian trees and shrubs with toothed leaves and apetalous, wind-pollinated flowers.

taxa.name: Papaveraceae
taxa.blurb: Poppy family: herbs with often colored latex or sap; showy flowers with soon-falling sepals; source of opium and many ornamentals.

taxa.name: Circaeasteraceae
taxa.blurb: Tiny family of two Asian alpine herbs with dichotomously veined leaves, a rare trait among flowering plants.

taxa.name: Lardizabalaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly climbing woody vines with palmately compound leaves and pulpy edible fruits, native to Asia and Chile.

taxa.name: Menispermaceae
taxa.blurb: Moonseed family: mainly tropical woody climbers with crescent-shaped seeds; several yield alkaloids such as curare and medicinal compounds.

taxa.name: Berberidaceae
taxa.blurb: Barberry family: shrubs and herbs often with yellow wood and alkaloids; flower parts typically in whorls of three.

taxa.name: Ranunculaceae
taxa.blurb: Buttercup family: mostly herbaceous, often with numerous spirally arranged parts; many are toxic and grown as ornamentals.

taxa.name: Sabiaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical and subtropical trees, shrubs, and climbers with small flowers whose parts occur in fives.

taxa.name: Nelumbonaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Nelumbo: aquatic herbs with large peltate leaves and showy flowers, superficially resembling but not related to water lilies.

taxa.name: Platanaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Platanus: large deciduous trees with flaking bark, palmately lobed leaves, and dense spherical flower heads.

taxa.name: Proteaceae
taxa.blurb: Protea family: mostly Southern Hemisphere shrubs and trees with clustered flowers and often leathery leaves; includes the edible macadamia nut.

taxa.name: Trochodendraceae
taxa.blurb: Two East Asian tree genera notable for wood that lacks vessels, a primitive trait retained among flowering plants.

taxa.name: Buxaceae
taxa.blurb: Boxwood family: evergreen shrubs and trees with simple opposite leaves and small unisexual flowers; widely used as hedging.

taxa.name: Myrothamnaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Myrothamnus: African and Madagascan desiccation-tolerant shrubs that revive after drying, with fan-shaped folding leaves.

taxa.name: Gunneraceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Gunnera: herbs ranging from tiny to giant-leaved, hosting nitrogen-fixing Nostoc cyanobacteria in their stems.

taxa.name: Dilleniaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly tropical trees, shrubs, and lianas with often showy yellow or white flowers and typically toothed leaves.

taxa.name: Peridiscaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of tropical trees from South America and Africa with numerous stamens and simple leaves.

taxa.name: Paeoniaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Paeonia: herbs and shrubs with large showy flowers and conspicuous fleshy fruits; widely grown as ornamentals.

taxa.name: Altingiaceae
taxa.blurb: Resinous trees with palmately lobed leaves and spiky spherical fruit clusters; source of the fragrant resin storax.

taxa.name: Hamamelidaceae
taxa.blurb: Witch-hazel family: shrubs and trees often with strap-shaped petals and star-shaped hairs; several flower in winter.

taxa.name: Cercidiphyllaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Cercidiphyllum: East Asian deciduous trees with rounded leaves that smell of caramel in autumn.

taxa.name: Daphniphyllaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Daphniphyllum: evergreen dioecious Asian trees and shrubs with leathery leaves and distinctive alkaloids.

taxa.name: Iteaceae
taxa.blurb: Shrubs and small trees with fragrant flowers in slender racemes, valued as ornamentals; mainly Asian with a few American species.

taxa.name: Grossulariaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Ribes: shrubs, often spiny, bearing edible berries including currants and gooseberries.

taxa.name: Saxifragaceae
taxa.blurb: Saxifrage family: mostly perennial herbs of temperate and alpine regions with basal leaves and small five-parted flowers.

taxa.name: Crassulaceae
taxa.blurb: Stonecrop family: succulent herbs and shrubs using CAM photosynthesis, adapted to dry habitats and popular as houseplants.

taxa.name: Aphanopetalaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Aphanopetalum: two Australian woody climbers whose sepals enlarge and persist as papery wings on the fruit.

taxa.name: Tetracarpaeaceae
taxa.blurb: Single species Tetracarpaea tasmannica: a Tasmanian evergreen shrub with glossy toothed leaves and four separate carpels.

taxa.name: Penthoraceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Penthorum: marsh herbs of North America and East Asia with yellow-green flowers lacking petals.

taxa.name: Haloragaceae
taxa.blurb: Watermilfoil family: aquatic and marsh herbs with often finely divided submerged leaves; some are troublesome invasive weeds.

taxa.name: Cynomoriaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Cynomorium: leafless red parasitic herbs of arid regions, long used in traditional medicine.

taxa.name: Vitaceae
taxa.blurb: Grape family: mostly tendril-climbing vines with leaf-opposed tendrils and berry fruits; includes the wine grape.

taxa.name: Krameriaceae
taxa.blurb: Ratany family: hemiparasitic desert shrubs whose roots tap neighbors, with one genus and oil-secreting flowers pollinated by bees.

taxa.name: Zygophyllaceae
taxa.blurb: Caltrop family: drought-tolerant shrubs and herbs of arid lands, including desert creosote bush and medicinal lignum vitae.

taxa.name: Quillajaceae
taxa.blurb: Soapbark family: South American evergreen trees whose saponin-rich bark yields commercial soap and vaccine adjuvants.

taxa.name: Fabaceae
taxa.blurb: Legume family: nitrogen-fixing plants bearing pod fruits; third-largest plant family and a cornerstone of agriculture.

taxa.name: Surianaceae
taxa.blurb: Small pantropical family of coastal shrubs and trees, typified by the salt-tolerant bay cedar of sandy tropical shores.

taxa.name: Polygalaceae
taxa.blurb: Milkwort family: herbs, shrubs and vines with pea-like flowers, once thought to boost milk yield in grazing livestock.

taxa.name: Rosaceae
taxa.blurb: Rose family: temperate trees, shrubs and herbs including apples, cherries, strawberries and roses; major source of fruit crops.

taxa.name: Barbeyaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-species family of one wind-pollinated tree native to dry highlands of the Horn of Africa and Arabia.

taxa.name: Dirachmaceae
taxa.blurb: Tiny family of two shrubs from Socotra and Somalia, notable for rose-like flowers with unusual hairy petals.

taxa.name: Elaeagnaceae
taxa.blurb: Oleaster family: nitrogen-fixing shrubs with silvery scaly leaves, including sea buckthorn and Russian olive.

taxa.name: Rhamnaceae
taxa.blurb: Buckthorn family: trees and shrubs, often thorny, including jujube fruit, California lilac and dye-yielding buckthorns.

taxa.name: Ulmaceae
taxa.blurb: Elm family: deciduous trees with asymmetric leaf bases and winged samara fruits, valued as shade and timber trees.

taxa.name: Cannabaceae
taxa.blurb: Hemp family: includes cannabis, hops used to flavor and preserve beer, and hackberry trees.

taxa.name: Moraceae
taxa.blurb: Mulberry family: often latex-bearing trees with tiny flowers in fleshy clusters, including figs, mulberries and breadfruit.

taxa.name: Urticaceae
taxa.blurb: Nettle family: herbs and shrubs, many with stinging hairs, including stinging nettle and the fibre plant ramie.

taxa.name: Nothofagaceae
taxa.blurb: Southern beech family: Southern Hemisphere trees dominating temperate forests of Chile, New Zealand and Australia.

taxa.name: Fagaceae
taxa.blurb: Beech family: temperate trees bearing nuts in cups or burs, including oaks, beeches and chestnuts; key forest and timber trees.

taxa.name: Myricaceae
taxa.blurb: Bayberry family: aromatic nitrogen-fixing shrubs, including bayberry and sweet gale, with wax-coated fruits.

taxa.name: Juglandaceae
taxa.blurb: Walnut family: deciduous trees with aromatic compound leaves and edible nuts, including walnuts, pecans and hickories.

taxa.name: Casuarinaceae
taxa.blurb: She-oak family: nitrogen-fixing trees with jointed green twigs resembling conifers, native to Australasia and the Pacific.

taxa.name: Ticodendraceae
taxa.blurb: Single-species family of one Central American cloud-forest tree, described only in 1989 and related to birches.

taxa.name: Betulaceae
taxa.blurb: Birch family: temperate trees and shrubs bearing catkins, including birches, alders, hazels and hornbeams.

taxa.name: Apodanthaceae
taxa.blurb: Parasitic family living entirely inside host stems, emerging only as tiny flowers that erupt through the bark.

taxa.name: Anisophylleaceae
taxa.blurb: Small tropical family of trees and shrubs with strikingly unequal leaves, found across Africa, Asia and the Americas.

taxa.name: Corynocarpaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of Australasian and Pacific trees, including the New Zealand karaka with edible but poisonous kernels.

taxa.name: Coriariaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-genus family of nitrogen-fixing shrubs with poisonous fruits, scattered across warm temperate regions worldwide.

taxa.name: Cucurbitaceae
taxa.blurb: Gourd family: tendril-climbing vines bearing fleshy fruits, including cucumbers, melons, squashes and pumpkins.

taxa.name: Tetramelaceae
taxa.blurb: Small Indo-Pacific family of two giant deciduous rainforest trees valued for their lightweight timber.

taxa.name: Datiscaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of tall dioecious herbs; the Eurasian bastard hemp yields a yellow dye.

taxa.name: Begoniaceae
taxa.blurb: Begonia family: succulent herbs with asymmetric leaves and separate male and female flowers, widely grown as ornamentals.

taxa.name: Lepidobotryaceae
taxa.blurb: Tiny family of two tropical tree species, one African and one Central American, with jointed leaf stalks.

taxa.name: Celastraceae
taxa.blurb: Bittersweet family: trees, shrubs and vines, including spindle trees, staff vines and the stimulant shrub khat.

taxa.name: Huaceae
taxa.blurb: Small African family of trees and lianas whose crushed bark and leaves smell strongly of garlic.

taxa.name: Connaraceae
taxa.blurb: Pantropical family of shrubs, lianas and trees, many with toxic or medicinal seeds; some yield zebrawood timber.

taxa.name: Oxalidaceae
taxa.blurb: Wood sorrel family: herbs and trees with sour clover-like leaves, including edible star fruit (carambola).

taxa.name: Cunoniaceae
taxa.blurb: Southern Hemisphere family of trees and shrubs concentrated in Australasia, many with fern-like compound leaves.

taxa.name: Elaeocarpaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical and Southern family of trees and shrubs with fringed petals, including blue-fruited quandongs.

taxa.name: Cephalotaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-species family comprising the Albany pitcher plant, a carnivorous herb of southwestern Australia.

taxa.name: Brunelliaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-genus family of tropical American cloud-forest trees with softly hairy, fern-like compound leaves.

taxa.name: Pandaceae
taxa.blurb: Small paleotropical family of trees and shrubs, mostly African, some yielding oil-rich edible drupes.

taxa.name: Irvingiaceae
taxa.blurb: Wild mango family: tropical African and Asian trees, including African mango prized for its fat-rich edible seeds.

taxa.name: Ctenolophonaceae
taxa.blurb: Tiny family of two evergreen tree species found in African and Southeast Asian rainforests.

taxa.name: Rhizophoraceae
taxa.blurb: Mangrove family: coastal trees, many stilt-rooted mangroves whose seeds germinate while still on the parent tree.

taxa.name: Erythroxylaceae
taxa.blurb: Coca family: tropical shrubs and small trees, including coca, the source of the alkaloid cocaine.

taxa.name: Ochnaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical family of trees and shrubs with glossy leaves and often bright fruits on swollen red receptacles.

taxa.name: Bonnetiaceae
taxa.blurb: Small Neotropical and Malesian family of trees and shrubs of tepui highlands and tropical heath forests.

taxa.name: Clusiaceae
taxa.blurb: Mangosteen family: tropical trees and shrubs with colored latex, including edible mangosteen and gamboge-yielding Garcinia.

taxa.name: Calophyllaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical family of latex-bearing trees and shrubs, including tamanu-oil Calophyllum and the mammee apple.

taxa.name: Podostemaceae
taxa.blurb: Riverweed family: aquatic herbs resembling algae or moss that cling to rocks in fast tropical rivers and waterfalls.

taxa.name: Hypericaceae
taxa.blurb: St. John's wort family: herbs, shrubs and trees with gland-dotted leaves, including medicinal St. John's wort.

taxa.name: Caryocaraceae
taxa.blurb: Small Neotropical family of trees and shrubs, including souari nut prized for its oil-rich edible kernels.

taxa.name: Lophopyxidaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-species family comprising one tendril-climbing woody vine of Southeast Asian and Pacific rainforests.

taxa.name: Putranjivaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical family of trees and shrubs with mustard-oil chemistry, including the Indian amulet-seed putranjiva.

taxa.name: Centroplacaceae
taxa.blurb: Tiny family of two tree genera, one African and one Asian, segregated from broader spurge relatives.

taxa.name: Elatinaceae
taxa.blurb: Waterwort family: small aquatic or wetland herbs of muddy margins, plus tropical shrubs of genus Bergia.

taxa.name: Malpighiaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical family of vines, shrubs and trees with oil-gland flowers, including Barbados cherry and the ayahuasca vine.

taxa.name: Balanopaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-genus family of dioecious evergreen trees of New Caledonia and eastern Australia, with acorn-like fruits.

taxa.name: Trigoniaceae
taxa.blurb: Small tropical family of trees, shrubs and lianas found in South America, Africa and Southeast Asia.

taxa.name: Dichapetalaceae
taxa.blurb: Poison-leaf family: tropical trees, shrubs and vines, some deadly from fluoroacetate used as arrow and rat poison.

taxa.name: Euphroniaceae
taxa.blurb: Tiny Neotropical family of three shrub or tree species from the Guiana Shield and Amazonian sandstone regions.

taxa.name: Chrysobalanaceae
taxa.blurb: Coco plum family: tropical trees and shrubs bearing drupes, including edible coco plum and oil-rich Licania.

taxa.name: Humiriaceae
taxa.blurb: Neotropical family of aromatic, resinous trees and shrubs, several yielding fragrant balsams and edible fruits.

taxa.name: Achariaceae
taxa.blurb: Chaulmoogra family: tropical plants including the source of chaulmoogra oil, historically used to treat leprosy.

taxa.name: Violaceae
taxa.blurb: Violet family: herbs, shrubs and trees, best known for violets and pansies with distinctive spurred flowers.

taxa.name: Goupiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-genus family of tall South American rainforest trees valued for their durable cupiuba timber.

taxa.name: Passifloraceae
taxa.blurb: Passionflower family: mostly tropical tendril climbers with intricate flowers, including passionfruit vines.

taxa.name: Lacistemataceae
taxa.blurb: Small Neotropical family of shrubs and small trees bearing tiny flowers in dense catkin-like spikes.

taxa.name: Salicaceae
taxa.blurb: Willow family: fast-growing trees and shrubs with catkins, including willows, poplars, aspens and cottonwoods.

taxa.name: Peraceae
taxa.blurb: Small pantropical family of latex-bearing trees and shrubs recently segregated from the spurge family.

taxa.name: Rafflesiaceae
taxa.blurb: Corpse flower family: leafless, stemless parasites producing the world's largest single flowers, which reek of carrion.

taxa.name: Euphorbiaceae
taxa.blurb: Spurge family: diverse plants with milky latex, including poinsettia, rubber tree, cassava and castor oil plant.

taxa.name: Linaceae
taxa.blurb: Flax family: herbs and shrubs, most notably flax, the source of linen fibre and linseed oil.

taxa.name: Ixonanthaceae
taxa.blurb: Small pantropical family of trees and shrubs, some yielding useful timber and resins.

taxa.name: Picrodendraceae
taxa.blurb: Pantropical family of trees and shrubs with often compound leaves, formerly grouped within the spurge family.

taxa.name: Phyllanthaceae
taxa.blurb: Leaf-flower family: tropical trees, shrubs and herbs, including Indian gooseberry (amla) and edible Antidesma berries.

taxa.name: Geraniaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and subshrubs with palmately lobed leaves and beaked, spring-loaded fruits; includes cranesbills and garden pelargoniums.

taxa.name: Francoaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of herbs, shrubs and trees, often with nectar-rich flowers, now including Melianthus and the honeybush Bersama.

taxa.name: Combretaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly tropical trees, shrubs and lianas, often with winged fruits; includes tropical almond, bushwillows and some mangroves.

taxa.name: Lythraceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs to trees with often four-parted flowers; includes purple loosestrife, crape myrtle, pomegranate and henna.

taxa.name: Onagraceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and shrubs typically with four-parted flowers and an inferior ovary; includes evening primroses, fuchsias and willowherbs.

taxa.name: Vochysiaceae
taxa.blurb: Neotropical and African trees and shrubs with often spurred, zygomorphic flowers, several yielding useful timber.

taxa.name: Myrtaceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic woody plants with oil-gland-dotted leaves and many stamens; includes eucalyptus, clove, guava and myrtle.

taxa.name: Melastomataceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly tropical plants with distinctive arcuate parallel leaf veins and curved anthers; includes princess flowers and many berries.

taxa.name: Crypteroniaceae
taxa.blurb: Small Indomalayan family of trees and shrubs with small wind-pollinated flowers in slender spikes or panicles.

taxa.name: Alzateaceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic family of a single Neotropical tree, Alzatea verticillata, with leathery leaves and small clustered flowers.

taxa.name: Penaeaceae
taxa.blurb: South African, mostly fynbos shrubs with small tubular flowers, now including the former Oliniaceae.

taxa.name: Aphloiaceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic family of a single evergreen shrub or tree of Africa, Madagascar and the Mascarenes with white many-stamened flowers.

taxa.name: Geissolomataceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic South African family of a heath-like shrub, Geissoloma marginatum, endemic to the Cape fynbos.

taxa.name: Strasburgeriaceae
taxa.blurb: Tiny family of evergreen trees from New Caledonia and New Zealand with large leathery leaves and showy solitary flowers.

taxa.name: Staphyleaceae
taxa.blurb: Trees and shrubs with compound leaves and inflated papery seed capsules, giving the bladdernuts their name.

taxa.name: Guamatelaceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic family of a single Central American shrub, Guamatela tuerckheimii, once placed with the roses.

taxa.name: Stachyuraceae
taxa.blurb: Single-genus East Asian family of shrubs bearing drooping spikes of small yellowish flowers before the leaves.

taxa.name: Crossosomataceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of arid-adapted North American shrubs with fleshy-arillate seeds, known as rockflowers.

taxa.name: Picramniaceae
taxa.blurb: Neotropical trees and shrubs with pinnate leaves and bitter tissues, formerly grouped with the quassia family.

taxa.name: Gerrardinaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-genus African family of shrubs and small trees with toothed leaves and small axillary flowers.

taxa.name: Petenaeaceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic family of a single Central American shrub or small tree, Petenaea cordata, with heart-shaped leaves.

taxa.name: Tapisciaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of trees with pinnate leaves and small flowers, disjunct between China and the Neotropics.

taxa.name: Dipentodontaceae
taxa.blurb: Small Asian and Neotropical family of shrubs and trees bearing umbel-like clusters of tiny flowers.

taxa.name: Biebersteiniaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-genus family of aromatic perennial herbs of arid western and central Asia with dissected leaves.

taxa.name: Nitrariaceae
taxa.blurb: Salt- and drought-tolerant shrubs and herbs of arid regions; includes the fleshy-fruited Nitraria and Syrian rue.

taxa.name: Kirkiaceae
taxa.blurb: Small African family of deciduous trees with pinnate leaves, adapted to dry savanna and rocky habitats.

taxa.name: Burseraceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical resinous trees and shrubs with fragrant bark; source of frankincense, myrrh and copal.

taxa.name: Anacardiaceae
taxa.blurb: Trees and shrubs with resin canals, often irritant sap; includes mango, cashew, pistachio, sumac and poison ivy.

taxa.name: Sapindaceae
taxa.blurb: Diverse woody and climbing plants, many with saponins; includes maples, horse chestnuts, lychee and soapberries.

taxa.name: Rutaceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic trees, shrubs and herbs with gland-dotted leaves; includes citrus, rue and Sichuan pepper.

taxa.name: Simaroubaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical to temperate trees and shrubs with intensely bitter quassinoid compounds; includes tree-of-heaven.

taxa.name: Meliaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly tropical trees valued for timber and useful compounds; includes mahogany, neem and Spanish cedar.

taxa.name: Cytinaceae
taxa.blurb: Root parasites lacking chlorophyll, emerging as clusters of fleshy flowers on the roots of host shrubs.

taxa.name: Muntingiaceae
taxa.blurb: Small Neotropical family of trees; includes the fast-growing, sweet-fruited Jamaican cherry Muntingia calabura.

taxa.name: Neuradaceae
taxa.blurb: Prostrate herbs of arid Africa and Asia with woolly leaves and spiny or winged fruits adapted for desert dispersal.

taxa.name: Malvaceae
taxa.blurb: Often mucilaginous plants with showy five-parted flowers and fused stamens; includes cotton, cacao, okra and hibiscus.

taxa.name: Sphaerosepalaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of trees and shrubs endemic to Madagascar, with leathery leaves and rounded fruits.

taxa.name: Thymelaeaceae
taxa.blurb: Shrubs and trees with tough fibrous bark and tubular flowers; includes daphnes and agarwood-producing Aquilaria.

taxa.name: Bixaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical shrubs and trees with colored sap; includes annatto (Bixa), source of the orange food dye achiote.

taxa.name: Cistaceae
taxa.blurb: Shrubs and herbs of dry, sunny habitats with crumpled five-petalled flowers; includes rock-roses and sun-roses.

taxa.name: Sarcolaenaceae
taxa.blurb: Family of trees and shrubs endemic to Madagascar, characterized by a cup-like involucre around the flowers or fruits.

taxa.name: Dipterocarpaceae
taxa.blurb: Tall tropical timber trees dominating Southeast Asian rainforests, with winged fruits and aromatic resins.

taxa.name: Akaniaceae
taxa.blurb: Tiny family of trees with pinnate leaves, disjunct between eastern Australia and eastern Asia.

taxa.name: Tropaeolaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-genus family of succulent, often climbing herbs with peltate leaves and spurred, pungent flowers; the garden nasturtiums.

taxa.name: Moringaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-genus family of fast-growing trees with pinnate leaves and pungent tissues; Moringa is a nutritious food crop.

taxa.name: Caricaceae
taxa.blurb: Soft-wooded tropical trees with milky latex and large fleshy fruits; includes the papaya.

taxa.name: Limnanthaceae
taxa.blurb: Small North American family of low annual herbs of wet places; meadowfoam seed yields a stable specialty oil.

taxa.name: Setchellanthaceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic family of a single Mexican desert shrub, Setchellanthus caeruleus, with showy blue flowers and mustard-oil chemistry.

taxa.name: Koeberliniaceae
taxa.blurb: Family of nearly leafless, green spiny desert shrubs of the Americas known as crucifixion thorn.

taxa.name: Bataceae
taxa.blurb: Succulent, salt-tolerant shrubs of coastal and mangrove habitats, with fleshy leaves and mustard-oil chemistry.

taxa.name: Salvadoraceae
taxa.blurb: Shrubs and small trees of arid Africa and Asia; Salvadora persica twigs are used as natural toothbrushes.

taxa.name: Emblingiaceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic family of a single prostrate shrub endemic to Western Australia, with unusual slipper-shaped flowers.

taxa.name: Tovariaceae
taxa.blurb: Single-genus Neotropical family of strong-smelling herbs and soft shrubs with mustard-oil chemistry.

taxa.name: Pentadiplandraceae
taxa.blurb: Monotypic African family; Pentadiplandra brazzeana produces the intensely sweet protein brazzein.

taxa.name: Gyrostemonaceae
taxa.blurb: Australian shrubs and trees with mustard-oil chemistry, often colonizing disturbed or fire-cleared ground.

taxa.name: Resedaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and shrubs with fringed petals and often fragrant flowers; includes garden mignonette and dyer's weld.

taxa.name: Capparaceae
taxa.blurb: Shrubs and trees of warm regions with mustard-oil chemistry; flower buds of Capparis are pickled as capers.

taxa.name: Cleomaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and shrubs with palmate leaves and long-stamened flowers; includes the ornamental spider flowers.

taxa.name: Brassicaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs with four-petalled cross-shaped flowers and pungent mustard oils; includes cabbage, mustard, canola and cress.

taxa.name: Aextoxicaceae
taxa.blurb: Single evergreen tree of Chile's Valdivian rainforest with scaly indumentum; sole member Aextoxicon punctatum (olivillo).

taxa.name: Berberidopsidaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of woody climbers from Chile and eastern Australia with showy red flowers; type genus Berberidopsis.

taxa.name: Olacaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical trees and shrubs, many root hemiparasites, in the sandalwood order; type genus Olax.

taxa.name: Opiliaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical hemiparasitic trees, shrubs and lianas that tap host roots via haustoria; ~10 genera in the Old and New World.

taxa.name: Balanophoraceae
taxa.blurb: Non-photosynthetic root parasites forming fleshy, fungus-like inflorescences on host roots in tropical forests.

taxa.name: Santalaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly hemiparasitic trees, shrubs and mistletoes, including fragrant sandalwood; ~1,000 species worldwide.

taxa.name: Misodendraceae
taxa.blurb: Southern South American mistletoes parasitic on Nothofagus, with feathery plumed fruits for wind dispersal; one genus.

taxa.name: Schoepfiaceae
taxa.blurb: Hemiparasitic shrubs and herbs of the Americas segregated from Olacaceae; three genera in the sandalwood order.

taxa.name: Loranthaceae
taxa.blurb: Largest mistletoe family: aerial stem hemiparasites with brightly colored bird-pollinated flowers; ~1,000 species.

taxa.name: Frankeniaceae
taxa.blurb: Salt-tolerant heath-like herbs and subshrubs of coasts and deserts that excrete salt; largely the genus Frankenia.

taxa.name: Tamaricaceae
taxa.blurb: Salt-tolerant shrubs and trees of arid Eurasia and Africa with scale-like leaves and feathery flower spikes.

taxa.name: Plumbaginaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and shrubs of saline and arid habitats with papery calyces and salt-excreting glands; ~1,000 species.

taxa.name: Polygonaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs to trees with swollen nodes sheathed by an ochrea; includes buckwheat, rhubarb and sorrels; ~1,200 species.

taxa.name: Droseraceae
taxa.blurb: Carnivorous plants trapping insects with sticky tentacles or snap-traps; includes the Venus flytrap; ~250 species.

taxa.name: Nepenthaceae
taxa.blurb: Old World carnivorous vines whose leaf tips form fluid-filled pitcher traps; single genus Nepenthes, ~170 species.

taxa.name: Drosophyllaceae
taxa.blurb: Single Iberian and Moroccan carnivorous subshrub with sticky flypaper leaves; sole species Drosophyllum lusitanicum.

taxa.name: Dioncophyllaceae
taxa.blurb: West African lianas of three genera, one of which (Triphyophyllum) is part-time carnivorous with sticky glandular leaves.

taxa.name: Ancistrocladaceae
taxa.blurb: Old World tropical lianas that climb by hooked branch tips, yielding naphthylisoquinoline alkaloids; one genus.

taxa.name: Rhabdodendraceae
taxa.blurb: Neotropical trees and shrubs of the single genus Rhabdodendron, found in Amazonian and Guianan forests.

taxa.name: Simmondsiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single desert shrub of the American Southwest whose seeds yield jojoba wax; sole species Simmondsia chinensis.

taxa.name: Physenaceae
taxa.blurb: Madagascan endemic trees and shrubs of the single genus Physena; two species with wind-pollinated flowers.

taxa.name: Asteropeiaceae
taxa.blurb: Madagascar-endemic trees and shrubs of the single genus Asteropeia, often on nutrient-poor soils; ~8 species.

taxa.name: Macarthuriaceae
taxa.blurb: Australian herbs and subshrubs of the single genus Macarthuria, recently split from the mollugo alliance.

taxa.name: Microteaceae
taxa.blurb: Neotropical herbs and subshrubs of the single genus Microtea, segregated from Phytolaccaceae; ~10 species.

taxa.name: Caryophyllaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs with swollen nodes and often notched petals; includes carnations, campions and chickweeds; ~2,600 species.

taxa.name: Achatocarpaceae
taxa.blurb: Spiny dioecious shrubs and small trees of warm Americas with berry fruits; two genera formerly in Phytolaccaceae.

taxa.name: Amaranthaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and shrubs, many salt- and drought-tolerant, including beet, spinach, quinoa and amaranth; ~2,000+ species.

taxa.name: Stegnospermataceae
taxa.blurb: Shrubs and climbers of Mexico, Central America and the Caribbean of the single genus Stegnosperma; ~3 species.

taxa.name: Limeaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and subshrubs of Africa and southern Asia of the single genus Limeum, segregated from Molluginaceae.

taxa.name: Lophiocarpaceae
taxa.blurb: African and Asian herbs and subshrubs of two genera, often halophytic; segregated from the mollugo alliance.

taxa.name: Kewaceae
taxa.blurb: Succulent-leaved herbs of southern Africa, Madagascar and St Helena of the single genus Kewa (formerly Hypertelis).

taxa.name: Barbeuiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single woody liana endemic to Madagascar; sole species Barbeuia madagascariensis with large capsular fruits.

taxa.name: Gisekiaceae
taxa.blurb: Prostrate to erect herbs of arid Africa and Asia of the single genus Gisekia; some are C4 plants.

taxa.name: Aizoaceae
taxa.blurb: Leaf-succulents centered in southern Africa with daisy-like flowers; includes living stones; ~2,000+ species.

taxa.name: Phytolaccaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs, shrubs and trees with berry fruits and often toxic tissues; centered on the genus Phytolacca.

taxa.name: Petiveriaceae
taxa.blurb: Neotropical herbs and shrubs, some pungently scented, segregated from Phytolaccaceae; ~9 genera.

taxa.name: Sarcobataceae
taxa.blurb: Spiny, salt-tolerant desert shrubs of western North America; single genus Sarcobatus (greasewood), ~2 species.

taxa.name: Nyctaginaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs, shrubs and trees, often with colorful petal-like bracts; includes bougainvillea; ~400 species.

taxa.name: Molluginaceae
taxa.blurb: Small herbs, many C4 weeds of open ground, with whorled leaves; type genus Mollugo; ~90 species.

taxa.name: Montiaceae
taxa.blurb: Often succulent herbs of the Americas with showy flowers; includes bitterroot and miner's lettuce; ~225 species.

taxa.name: Didiereaceae
taxa.blurb: Spiny stem-succulent shrubs and trees of Madagascar's spiny forest and southern Africa; ~20 species.

taxa.name: Basellaceae
taxa.blurb: Fleshy twining vines of the tropics, some grown as leaf or tuber vegetables like ulluco; ~20 species.

taxa.name: Halophytaceae
taxa.blurb: Single succulent halophytic herb of Argentine salt flats; sole species Halophytum ameghinoi.

taxa.name: Talinaceae
taxa.blurb: Succulent herbs and subshrubs of warm regions with fleshy leaves and colorful flowers; type genus Talinum.

taxa.name: Portulacaceae
taxa.blurb: Now restricted to the succulent genus Portulaca; fleshy herbs with showy flowers, including purslane; ~120 species.

taxa.name: Anacampserotaceae
taxa.blurb: Small leaf-succulent herbs and cushion plants of arid regions, mainly southern Africa and the Americas; ~36 species.

taxa.name: Cactaceae
taxa.blurb: Stem-succulents of the Americas with spines (modified leaves) borne on areoles and water-storing tissue; ~1,750 species.

taxa.name: Nyssaceae
taxa.blurb: Trees of temperate and tropical Asia and North America, including tupelos and the handkerchief tree; ~5 genera.

taxa.name: Hydrostachyaceae
taxa.blurb: Submerged aquatic herbs of African and Madagascan rivers, anchored to rocks in fast water; single genus Hydrostachys.

taxa.name: Hydrangeaceae
taxa.blurb: Shrubs, trees and vines of the north temperate zone, many ornamental with showy sterile flowers; ~17 genera.

taxa.name: Loasaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and shrubs, mainly American, often clothed in stinging or barbed hairs; type genus Loasa; ~330 species.

taxa.name: Curtisiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single southern African evergreen tree valued for its timber; sole species Curtisia dentata (assegai).

taxa.name: Grubbiaceae
taxa.blurb: Heath-like evergreen shrubs endemic to South Africa's Cape fynbos; single genus Grubbia, ~3 species.

taxa.name: Cornaceae
taxa.blurb: Trees and shrubs, mostly north temperate, many with showy petal-like bracts; largely the genus Cornus.

taxa.name: Balsaminaceae
taxa.blurb: Succulent-stemmed herbs with spurred flowers and explosive seed capsules; ~1,000 species, mostly Impatiens.

taxa.name: Marcgraviaceae
taxa.blurb: Neotropical epiphytic shrubs and lianas with pitcher-like nectary bracts pollinated by birds and bats; ~130 species.

taxa.name: Tetrameristaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical trees of three genera in SE Asia and the Neotropics, including a mangrove (Pelliciera).

taxa.name: Fouquieriaceae
taxa.blurb: Spiny, drought-deciduous desert shrubs and trees of Mexico and the SW US; single genus Fouquieria; ~11 species.

taxa.name: Polemoniaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and some shrubs, centered in western North America, with showy tubular five-lobed flowers; ~400 species.

taxa.name: Lecythidaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly tropical trees with large woody fruits and showy flowers; includes the Brazil nut; ~350 species.

taxa.name: Sladeniaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical trees of two genera in Africa and southern Asia, allied to the tea family; few species.

taxa.name: Pentaphylacaceae
taxa.blurb: Evergreen trees and shrubs, mostly of tropical and subtropical Asia and the Americas; ~12 genera, ~340 species.

taxa.name: Sapotaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical trees with milky latex, edible fruits and useful oils; sources of chicle, shea and argan; ~1,100 species.

taxa.name: Ebenaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical and subtropical trees and shrubs; genus Diospyros yields ebony timber and persimmon fruit; ~750 species.

taxa.name: Primulaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs, shrubs and some trees with five-parted flowers; includes primroses and cyclamens; ~2,600 species.

taxa.name: Theaceae
taxa.blurb: Evergreen and deciduous trees and shrubs with showy flowers; includes tea and camellias; ~200 species.

taxa.name: Symplocaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical to warm-temperate trees and shrubs often accumulating aluminium; single genus Symplocos; ~300 species.

taxa.name: Diapensiaceae
taxa.blurb: Low evergreen herbs and cushion plants of arctic, alpine and temperate regions of the Northern Hemisphere; ~15 species.

taxa.name: Styracaceae
taxa.blurb: Trees and shrubs of warm temperate and tropical regions with pendulous white flowers; source of benzoin resin; ~160 species.

taxa.name: Sarraceniaceae
taxa.blurb: American carnivorous plants with leaves modified into fluid-filled pitcher traps; three genera, ~30+ species.

taxa.name: Roridulaceae
taxa.blurb: Southern African shrubs with resin-tipped sticky leaves that trap insects fed on by symbiotic bugs; genus Roridula, 2 species.

taxa.name: Actinidiaceae
taxa.blurb: Trees, shrubs and woody vines of Asia and the Americas; genus Actinidia yields kiwifruit; ~350 species.

taxa.name: Clethraceae
taxa.blurb: Trees and shrubs with fragrant white flower racemes, mainly Asia and the Americas; ~75 species.

taxa.name: Cyrillaceae
taxa.blurb: Evergreen or deciduous shrubs and trees of American wetlands with slender flower racemes; two genera.

taxa.name: Ericaceae
taxa.blurb: Shrubs and small trees, often of acidic soils, with mycorrhizal roots; heaths, rhododendrons and blueberries; ~4,000 species.

taxa.name: Mitrastemonaceae
taxa.blurb: Non-photosynthetic endoparasites living inside the roots of oaks and relatives, emerging only to flower; single genus Mitrastemon.

taxa.name: Oncothecaceae
taxa.blurb: Two evergreen shrub species of the single genus Oncotheca endemic to New Caledonia.

taxa.name: Icacinaceae
taxa.blurb: Pantropical trees, shrubs and lianas, some with edible starchy tubers or drupe fruits.

taxa.name: Metteniusaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical trees and shrubs assembled from several former Icacinaceae genera around type Metteniusa.

taxa.name: Eucommiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single Chinese deciduous tree Eucommia ulmoides yielding latex and prized medicinal bark.

taxa.name: Garryaceae
taxa.blurb: Evergreen dioecious shrubs, including tassel-flowered Garrya and spotted-leaved Aucuba.

taxa.name: Rubiaceae
taxa.blurb: Huge family of trees, shrubs and herbs with opposite leaves and stipules; source of coffee and quinine.

taxa.name: Gentianaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs of temperate and montane regions with often vivid blue, funnel-shaped bitter-tasting flowers.

taxa.name: Loganiaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical trees, shrubs and vines, several rich in potent alkaloids like strychnine.

taxa.name: Gelsemiaceae
taxa.blurb: Woody vines and shrubs including toxic Gelsemium, the Carolina and yellow jessamines.

taxa.name: Apocynaceae
taxa.blurb: Often milky-sapped herbs, shrubs and vines with paired follicles; includes oleander and milkweeds.

taxa.name: Boraginaceae
taxa.blurb: Bristly-haired herbs with coiled flower clusters and five-lobed blooms; includes borage and comfrey.

taxa.name: Vahliaceae
taxa.blurb: Small herbs of the single genus Vahlia from dry regions of Africa and Asia.

taxa.name: Convolvulaceae
taxa.blurb: Twining herbs and vines with funnel-shaped flowers; includes morning glory and sweet potato.

taxa.name: Solanaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs, shrubs and vines with five-lobed flowers and berries; includes potato, tomato and tobacco.

taxa.name: Montiniaceae
taxa.blurb: African and Madagascan shrubs and small trees with unisexual flowers, type genus Montinia.

taxa.name: Sphenocleaceae
taxa.blurb: Fleshy wetland and ricefield herbs of the single genus Sphenoclea with dense flower spikes.

taxa.name: Hydroleaceae
taxa.blurb: Wetland herbs and subshrubs of the single genus Hydrolea, often spiny with blue flowers.

taxa.name: Plocospermataceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Plocosperma of shrubs from Mexico and Central America.

taxa.name: Carlemanniaceae
taxa.blurb: Small herbs and shrubs of shaded Asian forests, comprising just two genera.

taxa.name: Oleaceae
taxa.blurb: Trees and shrubs with opposite leaves and four-lobed flowers; includes olive, ash, jasmine and lilac.

taxa.name: Tetrachondraceae
taxa.blurb: Two genera of small creeping or mat-forming herbs of wet ground and disturbed sites.

taxa.name: Calceolariaceae
taxa.blurb: Andean herbs and shrubs with inflated pouch-shaped, slipper-like two-lipped flowers.

taxa.name: Gesneriaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical herbs and shrubs with showy tubular flowers; includes African violet and gloxinia.

taxa.name: Plantaginaceae
taxa.blurb: Diverse herbs with two-lipped or spiked flowers; includes plantain, foxglove and snapdragon.

taxa.name: Scrophulariaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs and shrubs with two-lipped flowers; includes figwort, mullein and butterfly bush.

taxa.name: Stilbaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly South African shrubs, many heath-like fynbos plants, with small tubular flowers.

taxa.name: Linderniaceae
taxa.blurb: Small herbs of moist habitats with two-lipped flowers; includes the wishbone flower Torenia.

taxa.name: Byblidaceae
taxa.blurb: Carnivorous herbs of the single genus Byblis with sticky glandular leaves that trap insects.

taxa.name: Martyniaceae
taxa.blurb: Sticky-glandular herbs whose woody fruits bear long hooked horns, the devil's claws.

taxa.name: Pedaliaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs of warm dry regions, often mucilaginous or spiny-fruited; includes sesame and devil's claw.

taxa.name: Acanthaceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical herbs and shrubs with showy bracts and two-lipped flowers; includes bear's breeches.

taxa.name: Bignoniaceae
taxa.blurb: Trees, shrubs and lianas with large trumpet flowers; includes jacaranda and trumpet vine.

taxa.name: Lentibulariaceae
taxa.blurb: Carnivorous herbs trapping prey with bladders or sticky leaves; includes bladderworts and butterworts.

taxa.name: Schlegeliaceae
taxa.blurb: Neotropical lianas, shrubs and hemiepiphytes with tubular flowers, type genus Schlegelia.

taxa.name: Thomandersiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single African genus Thomandersia of tropical forest shrubs and small trees.

taxa.name: Verbenaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs, shrubs and trees, often aromatic, with clustered small flowers; includes verbena and lantana.

taxa.name: Lamiaceae
taxa.blurb: Aromatic herbs with square stems and two-lipped flowers; includes basil, lavender, sage and rosemary.

taxa.name: Mazaceae
taxa.blurb: Low herbs of Asia and Australasia with two-lipped flowers, small segregate of the figwort alliance.

taxa.name: Phrymaceae
taxa.blurb: Herbs with two-lipped flowers, including the colourful monkeyflowers and lopseed.

taxa.name: Paulowniaceae
taxa.blurb: Fast-growing trees, chiefly Paulownia, with large leaves and showy foxglove-like purple flowers.

taxa.name: Orobanchaceae
taxa.blurb: Mostly parasitic herbs tapping host roots; includes broomrape, witchweed and Indian paintbrush.

taxa.name: Stemonuraceae
taxa.blurb: Tropical trees and shrubs of the Old and New World, with drupe fruits often bearing a fleshy appendage; ~12 genera.

taxa.name: Cardiopteridaceae
taxa.blurb: Small pantropical family of trees, shrubs and the climbing vine Cardiopteris, in the holly order Aquifoliales.

taxa.name: Phyllonomaceae
taxa.blurb: Single Neotropical genus of shrubs whose tiny flower clusters are borne on the upper surface of the leaf blade.

taxa.name: Helwingiaceae
taxa.blurb: East Asian shrubs, the sole genus Helwingia, remarkable for flowers borne on the midrib of the leaf surface.

taxa.name: Aquifoliaceae
taxa.blurb: Holly family: a single large genus Ilex of evergreen trees and shrubs with bright berries and often spiny leaves.

taxa.name: Rousseaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of shrubs and trees ranging from Mauritius to Australasia; Roussea is a Mauritian bird-pollinated liana.

taxa.name: Campanulaceae
taxa.blurb: Bellflower family: mostly herbs with milky latex and bell- or star-shaped flowers; about 2,400 species worldwide.

taxa.name: Pentaphragmataceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus of fleshy, asymmetric-leaved herbs from the rainforest understorey of tropical Southeast Asia.

taxa.name: Stylidiaceae
taxa.blurb: Triggerplants: mostly Australian herbs whose touch-sensitive floral column snaps to dust pollinators with pollen.

taxa.name: Alseuosmiaceae
taxa.blurb: Southern-hemisphere shrubs of New Zealand, Australia and New Caledonia with fragrant tubular flowers; ~10 species.

taxa.name: Phellinaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus Phelline of trees and shrubs endemic to New Caledonia, allied to the hollies.

taxa.name: Argophyllaceae
taxa.blurb: Australasian and Pacific shrubs, many with silvery leaf undersides; includes the ornamental wire-netting bush Corokia.

taxa.name: Menyanthaceae
taxa.blurb: Aquatic and wetland herbs with fringed white or yellow flowers, including the floating-hearts and bogbean.

taxa.name: Goodeniaceae
taxa.blurb: Largely Australian family with distinctive fan-shaped flowers and a pollen-cup on the style; includes Scaevola.

taxa.name: Calyceraceae
taxa.blurb: South American herbs whose flowers cluster in compact heads, convergent with the daisies to which they are related.

taxa.name: Asteraceae
taxa.blurb: Daisy family: florets packed into composite heads that mimic single blooms; largest eudicot family, ~32,000 species.

taxa.name: Escalloniaceae
taxa.blurb: Shrubs and trees centred on South America and Australasia; Escallonia is widely grown as a hedging ornamental.

taxa.name: Columelliaceae
taxa.blurb: Two genera of Andean and Central American shrubs, including the holly-like, tubular-flowered Desfontainia.

taxa.name: Bruniaceae
taxa.blurb: Heath-like shrubs almost confined to the South African fynbos, with tiny leaves and flowers in dense heads.

taxa.name: Paracryphiaceae
taxa.blurb: Trees and shrubs of New Caledonia and eastern Australasia, uniting several formerly isolated genera.

taxa.name: Adoxaceae
taxa.blurb: Shrubs and herbs bearing flat or rounded flower clusters and berry fruits, including elders and viburnums.

taxa.name: Caprifoliaceae
taxa.blurb: Honeysuckle family: shrubs, vines and herbs with often tubular, nectar-rich flowers; includes teasels and valerians.

taxa.name: Pennantiaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus of trees and shrubs from New Zealand and eastern Australia; Pennantia corymbosa is the NZ kaikomako.

taxa.name: Torricelliaceae
taxa.blurb: Small family of trees and shrubs disjunct across the Himalayas, Southeast Asia and Madagascar.

taxa.name: Griseliniaceae
taxa.blurb: Single genus of evergreen shrubs and trees split between New Zealand and South America; Griselinia is a hedge plant.

taxa.name: Pittosporaceae
taxa.blurb: Old World and Australasian trees, shrubs and climbers, many with fragrant flowers and sticky-seeded capsules.

taxa.name: Araliaceae
taxa.blurb: Ivy and ginseng family: often woody plants with umbel inflorescences; includes ivy, ginseng and umbrella trees.

taxa.name: Myodocarpaceae
taxa.blurb: Two genera of trees and shrubs centred on New Caledonia, split from the ginseng family Araliaceae.

taxa.name: Apiaceae
taxa.blurb: Carrot family: aromatic herbs with tiny flowers in flat umbels and hollow stems; includes carrot, celery and parsley.
